name: io.github.SocioLogicAI/signal-relay
description: Connect AI agents to SocioLogic's synthetic persona platform for market research.

tools.name: sociologic_list_personas
tools.description: [Research/Personas] List available synthetic personas from the SocioLogic marketplace or your private collection. Use this to discover personas for interviews or campaigns.

tools.name: sociologic_get_persona
tools.description: [Research/Personas] Get detailed information about a specific persona including demographics, psychographics, and behavioral traits.

tools.name: sociologic_create_persona
tools.description: [Research/Personas] Create a new AI persona from a natural language description. Returns rich persona data including demographics, psychographics, communication style, and behavioral traits. Use the returned data to role-play as this persona in conversation, or pass it to a subagent for user research interviews.

tools.name: sociologic_get_persona_memories
tools.description: [Research/Personas] Retrieve a persona's semantic memories. Memories are vector-embedded learnings from past interactions that inform future responses.

tools.name: sociologic_list_campaigns
tools.description: [Research/Campaigns] List your research campaigns. Campaigns are structured multi-persona interview sessions with defined questions.

tools.name: sociologic_get_campaign
tools.description: [Research/Campaigns] Get detailed information about a specific campaign including status, personas, questions, and interview results.

tools.name: sociologic_create_campaign
tools.description: [Research/Campaigns] Create a new research campaign. Define questions and either generate new personas or use existing ones. Campaigns enable systematic multi-persona research.

tools.name: sociologic_execute_campaign
tools.description: [Research/Campaigns] Execute a draft campaign. This triggers background interviews with all personas and generates a research report. Long-running operation.

tools.name: sociologic_export_campaign
tools.description: [Research/Campaigns] Export a completed campaign's results as PDF or JSON. PDF includes executive summary, persona responses, and synthesized findings.

tools.name: sociologic_list_focus_groups
tools.description: [Research/Focus Groups] List your focus groups. Focus groups are collections of personas for cohort-based research.

tools.name: sociologic_get_focus_group
tools.description: [Research/Focus Groups] Get detailed information about a focus group including its member personas.

tools.name: sociologic_create_focus_group
tools.description: [Research/Focus Groups] Create a new focus group to organize personas for cohort-based research.

tools.name: sociologic_add_personas_to_focus_group
tools.description: [Research/Focus Groups] Add one or more personas to an existing focus group.

tools.name: sociologic_get_credits_balance
tools.description: [Payments] Check your current credits balance. Credits are used for persona interviews and campaign execution.

tools.name: sociologic_get_x402_discovery
tools.description: [Payments] Get x402 payment discovery information. Returns which endpoints accept crypto payments (USDC on Base), wallet address, pricing, and facilitator details.

tools.name: sociologic_search_web
tools.description: [Search & Scraping] Search the web and return scraped results. Returns markdown content from matching pages.

tools.name: sociologic_research_topic
tools.description: [Search & Scraping] Search a topic and aggregate content from multiple web sources. Returns summarized results useful for building research context.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_uuid
tools.description: [Utilities] Generate a cryptographically random UUID v4. Useful for creating unique identifiers for experiments, sessions, or tracking.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_random
tools.description: [Utilities] Generate a cryptographically random floating-point number between 0 and 1. Uses hardware entropy for true randomness. Returns a float. Use sociologic_rng_int for integers in a specific range.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_int
tools.description: [Utilities] Generate a cryptographically random integer within a range. Default range is 0-100. Uses hardware entropy for true randomness.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_dice
tools.description: [Utilities] Roll a cryptographically random die with a configurable number of sides. Default is a 6-sided die. Uses hardware entropy for true randomness.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_coin
tools.description: [Utilities] Flip a cryptographically random coin. Returns heads or tails. Uses hardware entropy for true randomness.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_shuffle
tools.description: [Utilities] Randomly shuffle an array of items using the Fisher-Yates algorithm with cryptographic randomness. Useful for randomizing survey question order, A/B test assignments, or persona selection.

tools.name: sociologic_rng_weighted
tools.description: [Utilities] Select a random item from an array using weighted probabilities with cryptographic randomness. Useful for simulating demographic distributions, market share splits, or probability-based scenarios.
